channel.title: batch on T++
channel.description: Recent content in batch on T++

item.title: Echo is on.
item.description: Batch script is old. Batch script is quirky, and has the syntax of the aborted love child of bash and perl. This shouldn’t be news to anyone, but if you use batch script as infrequently as I do I have something that might be news. Consider the following block of code if %PORT% == 80 ( CALL someScript.bat set error=%errorlevel% if %error% neq 0 exit /b %error% ) Batch script parses blocks, like the blocks inside an if statement, before it executes them.
